name: save-my-tokens
description: Semantic code graph for efficient codebase exploration. Provides symbol lookup, dependency analysis, impact assessment, dead code detection, cycle detection, coupling hotspots, module reports, and semantic search via the smt CLI.

skills.name: Symbol Definition
skills.description: What is this symbol? Returns signature, docstring, immediate callees. 1-hop lookup.

skills.name: Symbol Context
skills.description: What do I need to work on this? Returns symbol + N-hop callees + callers, cycles collapsed.
examples: What context do I need for GraphBuilder?

skills.name: Change Impact Analysis
skills.description: What breaks if I change this? Returns all callers grouped by distance.
examples: What breaks if I change Neo4jClient?

skills.name: Semantic Code Search
skills.description: Find symbols by meaning using local embeddings. No API calls.
examples: Find code related to cycle detection

skills.name: Graph Health Check
skills.description: Check graph freshness, node/edge counts, and git commit alignment. Always run at session start.

skills.name: Caller Lookup
skills.description: Who calls this symbol? Uses context --callers flag for callers-only traversal.

skills.name: Multi-Agent Analysis Harness
skills.description: Single-agent pipeline for deep codebase analysis: pre-flight, query, reason, report. Handles impact prediction, isolation finding, and dependency mapping.
examples: What breaks if I refactor CallAnalyzer?
What parts can be worked on independently?

skills.name: Symbol Listing
skills.description: Enumerate all symbols in the graph, optionally filtered by module/file path substring.

skills.name: Dead Code Detection
skills.description: Find symbols with no callers — candidates for dead code removal. Entry points and public APIs are expected to appear here.

skills.name: Circular Dependency Detection
skills.description: Detect all circular dependencies in the call graph. Uses Tarjan's SCC algorithm, same as context/impact cycle collapse.

skills.name: Coupling Hotspots
skills.description: Most-called symbols ranked by unique caller count. High coupling = high refactoring risk.
examples: What are the most coupled functions?

skills.name: Dependency Path
skills.description: Shortest dependency path between two symbols via CALLS edges. Useful for understanding indirect coupling.
examples: How does A depend on B?
Find path from GraphBuilder to Neo4jClient

skills.name: Module Coupling Report
skills.description: Files ranked by symbol count and cross-file CALLS coupling. Identifies architectural hotspots at the file level.

skills.name: Git Change Impact
skills.description: Symbols in git-changed files with caller counts, pinpointed by line range. The essential command for PR review — fuses git diff with graph impact in one shot.

skills.name: God Function Detector
skills.description: Ranks symbols by fan-in × fan-out complexity score. Identifies 'god functions' that are both hard to refactor (many callees) and high blast-radius (many callers).

skills.name: File Surface Analysis
skills.description: File-level view: what a file exports (called by other files), what it imports (calls into other files), and which symbols are internal. Bridges file-vs-symbol exploration.

skills.name: Architectural Bottleneck
skills.description: Symbols that bridge distinct file clusters. Bridge score = (distinct caller files) × (distinct callee files) via cross-file edges. High score = structural chokepoint — removing this requires coordinating across subsystems.

skills.name: Architecture Layer Guard
skills.description: Detects forbidden dependency directions between architectural layers (e.g. parsers calling CLI). Configured via .smt_layers.json. Returns non-zero exit code on violations — safe for CI pipelines.
